scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced normal franchise execution only when item, supplier, price, inventory, and unit-level closure evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing operations-manual, supplier, item, and price-book prerequisites produced a blocked exception state instead of improvised progression.
The lane refused completion without auditable unit-level evidence and preserved the open or rework state.
A non-approved supplier substitution was treated as an approval-bound boundary case and routed out of lane.
Unsupported price and fee override requests were refused and escalated without the lane inventing commercial authority.
Conflicting POS, OMS, ERP, receiving, and inventory records stayed unresolved and were moved into exception handling rather than masked as successful execution.
Promotion mismatches were handled as explicit rework with owner routing instead of being closed as a routine store issue.
Agreement and state-addendum interpretation requests were stopped at the operations boundary and routed to a more specialized adjacent lane.
Franchise-specific terminology and artifact handling stayed grounded in PLU, GTIN, approved supplier, promo window, and protected-territory records.
Missing tenant facts and system mappings triggered low-confidence escalation instead of a confident but unsupported answer.
Conflicting FTC, state, and tenant policy signals were preserved as an escalation rather than synthesized into a unilateral price or fee answer.
Tenant-specific stricter controls were applied without displacing the public franchise, pricing, and item-data baseline.

notes: Scenario suite covers the queue-level must-pass families for incomplete inputs, execution exceptions requiring rework or escalation, and refusal to mark work complete without evidence.
All required shared, execution-phase, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios with complete coverage.
